original_assessment.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. From Azure Home, select the Portal Menu.
2. Select `Microsoft Entra ID`.
3. Under Manage, select `Users`.
4. Under Manage, select `Password reset`.
5. Under Manage, select `Notifications`.
6. Verify that the setting for `Notify all admins when other admins reset their password?` is set to `Yes`.
7. If the setting is set to `No`, this indicates a non-compliant configuration.

**Expected secure configuration:** 
The option `Notify all admins when other admins reset their password?` should be set to `Yes` to ensure all Global Administrators receive notifications about password resets.

**How to confirm compliance:** 
Confirm that each Global Administrator accounts for their notifications and has the setting correctly configured as stated above.

review.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** DISAGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as "Manual" is not entirely correct. This control can be audited using Azure PowerShell or Azure CLI, which would allow for an automated approach. Specifically, Azure PowerShell cmdlets like `Get-AzureADDirectorySetting` can be used to check the configuration of password reset notifications. Additionally, Azure Graph API or Microsoft Graph API can be leveraged to retrieve directory settings programmatically.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 6

- The manual steps provided are generally accurate for a manual check via the Azure Portal. However, they lack detail on how to navigate to the specific setting within the Azure Portal. The steps also do not account for potential differences in UI or permissions that might affect visibility of settings.
- The expected secure configuration is correctly identified, but the manual verification process could be more detailed, including screenshots or specific UI element names.
- The naming convention for a program is not applicable here as this is a manual audit process.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Automation Potential:** Highlight the possibility of using Azure PowerShell or Azure CLI for automation, which would improve efficiency and reduce human error.
- **Detailed Instructions:** Provide more detailed instructions on how to navigate the Azure Portal, including any prerequisites or permissions required to view or modify the settings.
- **Consideration of API Usage:** Include a section on how to use Azure PowerShell or CLI to automate this check, with example commands or scripts.
- **Notification Verification:** Consider adding steps to verify that notifications are actually being received by Global Administrators, not just that the setting is enabled.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- While the manual steps are generally correct, the lack of consideration for automation and incomplete instructions reduce confidence in the assessment's comprehensiveness.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Re-classify the audit approach to "Automated" with justification. Provide guidance on using Azure PowerShell or Azure CLI to automate the compliance check.
- Enhance the manual steps with more detailed navigation instructions and consider including a verification step for notification receipt.
- Include example scripts or commands for using Azure PowerShell or CLI to automate this compliance check.

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Notify all admins when other admins reset their password?' is set to 'Yes'
input_row.description: Ensure that all Global Administrators are notified if any other administrator resets their password.
input_row.rationale: Administrator accounts are sensitive. Any password reset activity notification, when sent to all Administrators, ensures that all Global Administrators can passively confirm if such a reset is a common pattern within their group. For example, if all Administrators change their password every 30 days, any password reset activity before that may require administrator(s) to evaluate any unusual activity and confirm its origin. Impact: All Global Administrators will receive a notification from Azure every time a password is reset. This is useful for auditing procedures to confirm that there are no out of the ordinary password resets for Administrators. There is additional overhead, however, in the time required for Global Administrators to audit the notifications. This setting is only useful if all Global Administrators pay attention to the notifications and audit each one.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select Password reset 5. Under Manage, select Notifications 6. Ensure that Notify all admins when other admins reset their password? is set to Yes
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select Password reset 5. Under Manage, select Notifications 6. Set Notify all admins when other admins reset their password? to Yes 7. Click Save Default Value: By default, Notify all admins when other admins reset their password? is set to "No". References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-sspr- howitworks#notifications 2. https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/account-billing/reset-your-work-or-school- password-using-security-info-23dde81f-08bb-4776-ba72-e6b72b9dda9e 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-governance- strategy#gs-6-define-and-implement-identity-and-privileged-access-strategy 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-privileged- access#pa-1-separate-and-limit-highly-privilegedadministrative-users 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/tutorial-enable- sspr#set-up-notifications-and-customizations
input_row.impact: All Global Administrators will receive a notification from Azure every time a password is reset. This is useful for auditing procedures to confirm that there are no out of the ordinary password resets for Administrators. There is additional overhead, however, in the time required for Global Administrators to audit the notifications. This setting is only useful if all Global Administrators pay attention to the notifications and audit each one.
